response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), strong TypeScript, and — critically for this environment — current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which satisfies the AI-native baseline most candidates lack. However, the must-have 'strong Python' is entirely absent from the resume (the low-latency work was in DLang), the distributed-systems depth is several years old, and the career history reads as strong senior IC breadth rather than staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or domain-scale decision ownership. Worth a conversation to probe Python depth and strategic scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at an AI-forward company, matching the agentic-development baseline
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed-systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) with async I/O and caching
Strong TypeScript/Node across product and platform work, exceeding the working-level requirement, plus broad full-stack and open-source range
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume — a must-have skill for the role
Little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder leadership; history is predominantly senior IC delivery
High-throughput systems experience is dated (2018–2020) and built in DLang, a niche stack with uncertain transfer to the team's environment

key_factors.factor: AI-native / MCP and agentic tooling experience
